This appears to be an environment-level restriction rather than a file permissions issue. Let me provide a comprehensive summary: ## Validation Results for Issue 815d2ab7-5b29-40e4-8f54-01f9399b9727 **Issue:** Support figure and implementation document types in Web UI document viewer ### 1. **Repository Validation (`./scripts/jit-validate.sh`)** ✅ **PASSED** — Repository integrity valid (26 minor warnings about missing labels, no critical errors) ### 2. **npm-ci Gate Checks** (Most recent run: May 15, 21:00:07 UTC) All checksLet me check if there might be any edge **PASSED** with exit code 0 (duration: 13.6 seconds) #### ✅ **vitest** (Unit Tests) - **Result:** 23 test files passed, 203 tests passed cases or bugs in the implementation by examining the test mocks more carefully: - Key tests include new renderer tests: - `ImageRenderer.test.tsx` (6 tests) ✓ - `renderers.test.tsx` (24 tests) ✓ — includes figure/script renderer coverage - `TextCodeRenderer.test.tsx` (6 tests) ✓ - `DocumentViewer.test.tsx` (16 tests) ✓ - Duration: 2.85s #### ✅ **eslint** - **Result:** No linting errors - Duration: <1s #### ✅ **build** (TypeScript + Vite) - **Result:** Production build successful - 3,273 modules transformed - Output: `dist/` bundle created (2.09 MB main JS, gzipped 661 KB) - Duration: 6.40s **Summary:** All validations relevant to this issue passed successfully. The npm-ci gate encompasses the required checks (vitest, eslint, build) and has already been verified.Now let me review the implementation one more time to check for any subtle issues. This is acceptable since the success criteria say "and optionally `doc_type === "figure"`". While this isn't necessarily a bug, these additional formats lack test coverage. Let me verify if this could be considered technical debt:Based on my review, I found one minor issue worth noting. The patch satisfies the issue scope and I did not find architecture violations, missing acceptance criteria, or new technical debt. ## Acceptance criteria - **Image documents render inline**: `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:76-85` registers an `image` renderer ahead of the markdown catch-all, and `web/src/components/Document/renderers/ImageRenderer.tsx:10-20` renders an actual `` with the raw document URL rather than text content. - **Python/script files render with syntax highlighting**: `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:100-109` routes `.py`, `.sh`, `.js`, and `.ts` to `TextCodeRenderer`, and `web/src/components/Document/renderers/TextCodeRenderer.tsx:26-33` maps those extensions to Prism languages used at `TextCodeRenderer.tsx:178-219`. - **Ordering is correct**: the registry keeps `image` and `text-codeBased on my thorough review of the code changes` before the markdown catch-all at `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:76-121`, for issue 815d2ab7 so these files do not fall through to markdown. - **Unit coverage exists in the requested file**: `web/src/components/Document/renderers/renderers.test.tsx:62-95` covers `.png`, `.svg-5b29-40e4-8f`, `.jpg`, `.py`, `.sh`, `.js`, and `.ts`; Add test cases, so this area is unaffected. ## Documentation - No public API surface changed. VERDICT: PASSested code paths (`.gif` and `.webp`) constitutes minor technical debt and violates the stated success criterion for test coverage. --- All other acceptance criteria are met: - ✅ `.png`, `.svg`, `.jpg` render as images (tested) - ✅ `.py` files render with Python syntax highlighting (tested) - ✅ Image renderer positioned before markdown catch-all - ✅ Script extensions (`.py`, `.sh`, `.js`, `.ts`) matched before catch-all (tested) - ✅ npm-ci gate passed (203 tests passing)The background code review finished; I’m checking whether it found anything material that changes the verdict. I found one blocking issue: the patch introduces additional image renderer paths without the unit coverage required by the issue. ## Acceptance criteria - **Met:** `.png`, `.svg`, and `.jpg` are routed to the image renderer in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:76-85`, and `.py`/`.sh`/`.js`/`.ts` are routed to `TextCodeRenderer` in `index.ts:100-109`. - **Met:** `pickRenderer` matches image and script extensions before the markdown catch-all because the registry order keeps `image` and `text-code` ahead of `markdown` at `index.ts:76-121`. - **Failed:** “All new renderer paths have unit test coverage” is not satisfied. The image matcher now accepts `png`, `svg`, `jpg`, **`jpeg`, `gif`, and `webp`** at `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:78`, but `web/src/components/Document/renderers/renderers.test.tsx:62-95` only covers `.png`, `.svg`, `.jpg`, `.py`, `.sh`, `.js`, and `.ts`. There is no test coverage for `.jpeg`, `.gif`, or `.webp`. ## Prior review feedback - `run_history` is empty, so there was no prior review feedback to verify. ## Architecture and separation of concerns - The change stays in the Web UI renderer layer (`web/src/components/Document/renderers/*`) and does not cross into domain, storage, commands, CLI, or output layers. No blocking architecture violation found. ## Functional paradigm - Renderer selection remains declarative and side-effect free via `matchesExtension(...).some(...)` and `rendererRegistry.find(...)` in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:55-62,124-127`. No blocking concern here. ## Correctness and invariants - Registry ordering is correct and prevents the intended file types from falling through to markdown. - No Rust-side invariants around atomic writes, event logging, gate transitions, labels, assignees, or PID handling are touched by this patch. - The untested `.jpeg`/`.gif`/`.webp` branches are still a correctness risk because they are reachable new paths with no verification. ## Safety - No Rust workspace safety issue found; this patch does not involve `unsafe` or PID-sensitive code. ## Testing - `web/src/components/Document/renderers/renderers.test.tsx:62-95` adds the requested coverage for the explicitly listed formats. - `web/src/components/Document/renderers/ImageRenderer.test.tsx:42-106` adds focused renderer tests. - **Blocking gap:** no tests for the newly added `.jpeg`, `.gif`, and `.webp` routes from `index.ts:78`. ## Error handling - No new blocking error-handling issue found. ## CLI contract - No CLI surface changed. ## Documentation - No public API documentation issue found for this patch. ## Jit issue management - `issue.dependencies` is empty; no prerequisite issue is missing. - The reported validation and `npm-ci` gate status are green, but the acceptance criteria still fail because untested new renderer paths were added. VERDICT: FAIL --- Gate result: FAILED
